spell.definition: Words or a formula supposed to have magical powers.

sphere.definition: A regular three-dimensional object in which every cross-section is a circle; the figure described by the revolution of a circle about its diameter .

spine.definition: The series of bones situated at the back from the head to the pelvis of a person, or from the head to the tail of an animal; backbone, vertebral column.

spotlight.definition: A bright, directional light or lamp, especially one used to illuminate the focus or center of attention on a stage.

spouse.definition: A person in a marriage or marital relationship.

spy.definition: A person who secretly watches and examines the actions of other individuals or organizations and gathers information on them (usually to gain an advantage).

squad.definition: A group of people organized for some common purpose, usually of about ten members.

stab.definition: An act of stabbing or thrusting with an object.

stability.definition: The condition of being stable or in equilibrium, and thus resistant to change.

stake.definition: A piece of wood or other material, usually long and slender, pointed at one end so as to be easily driven into the ground as a marker or a support or stay.

standing.definition: (heading) To position or be positioned physically.

steer.definition: A suggestion about a course of action.

stem.definition: The stock of a family; a race or generation of progenitors.

stereotype.definition: A conventional, formulaic, and often oversimplified or exaggerated conception, opinion, or image of (a person).

stimulus.definition: Any external phenomenon that has an influence on a system, by triggering or modifying an internal phenomenon.

stir.definition: The act or result of stirring (moving around the particles of a liquid etc.)

storage.definition: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.

straightforward.definition: Proceeding in a straight course or manner; not deviating.

strand.definition: The shore or beach of the sea or ocean; shore; beach.

stumble.definition: A fall, trip or substantial misstep.

submission.definition: The act of submitting or yielding; surrender.

subscriber.definition: A person who subscribes to a publication or a service

subscription.definition: Access to a resource for a period of time, generally for payment.

subsidy.definition: Financial support or assistance, such as a grant.

substantial.definition: Anything having substance; an essential part.

substantially.definition: In a strong substantial manner; considerably.

substitute.definition: A replacement or stand-in for something that achieves a similar result or purpose.

substitution.definition: The act of substituting or the state of being substituted.

subtle.definition: Hard to grasp; not obvious or easily understood; barely noticeable.

suburban.definition: A person who lives in a suburb.

succession.definition: An act of following in sequence.

successive.definition: Coming one after the other in a series.

successor.definition: A person or thing that immediately follows another in holding an office or title.

suck.definition: An instance of drawing something into one's mouth by inhaling.

sue.definition: To file a legal action against someone, generally a non-criminal action.

suite.definition: A retinue or company of attendants, as of a distinguished personage

summit.definition: A peak; the topmost point or surface, as of a mountain.

superb.definition: First-rate; of the highest quality; exceptionally good.

superintendent.definition: A person who is authorized to supervise, direct or administer something.

superior.definition: A person of higher rank or quality.

supervise.definition: To oversee or direct a task or organization.

supervision.definition: The act or instance of supervising.

supervisor.definition: A person with the official task of overseeing the work of a person or group, or of other operations and activities.

supplement.definition: Something added, especially to make up for a deficiency.

supposedly.definition: As a matter of supposition; in the beliefs or according to the claims of some people.

suppress.definition: To put an end to, especially with force, to crush, do away with; to prohibit, subdue.

surge.definition: A sudden transient rush, flood or increase.

surgical.definition: Of, relating to, used in, or resulting from surgery.

surplus.definition: That which remains when use or need is satisfied, or when a limit is reached; excess; overplus.

surrender.definition: An act of surrendering, submission into the possession of another; abandonment, resignation.

surveillance.definition: Close observation of an individual or group; person or persons under suspicion.

suspension.definition: The act of suspending, or the state of being suspended.

suspicion.definition: The act of suspecting something or someone, especially of something wrong.

sustain.definition: A mechanism which can be used to hold a note, as the right pedal on a piano.

swing.definition: The manner in which something is swung.

sword.definition: A long-bladed weapon with a hilt, and usually a pommel and cross-guard, which is designed to stab, slash, and/or hack.

syndrome.definition: A recognizable pattern of signs, symptoms and/or behaviours, especially of a disease or medical or psychological condition.

synthesis.definition: The formation of something complex or coherent by combining simpler things.

systematic.definition: Carried out using a planned, ordered procedure.

tackle.definition: A device for grasping an object and an attached means of moving it, as a rope and hook.

tactic.definition: A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.

taxpayer.definition: A person who is subject to, liable for, or pays tax as opposed to a nontaxpayer who is neither the subject nor the object of revenue laws.

tempt.definition: To provoke someone to do wrong, especially by promising a reward; to entice.

tenant.definition: One who pays a fee (rent) in return for the use of land, buildings, or other property owned by others.

tenure.definition: A status of possessing a thing or an office; an incumbency.

terminate.definition: To end, especially in an incomplete state.

terrain.definition: A single, distinctive rock formation; an area having a preponderance of a particular rock or group of rocks.

terrific.definition: Terrifying, causing terror; terrible; sublime, awe-inspiring.

testify.definition: To make a declaration, or give evidence, under oath.

testimony.definition: Statements made by a witness in court.

texture.definition: The feel or shape of a surface or substance; the smoothness, roughness, softness, etc. of something.

thankfully.definition: In a thankful manner; giving thanks.

theatrical.definition: A stage performance, especially one by amateurs.

theology.definition: The study of God, a god, or gods; and of the truthfulness of religion in general.

theoretical.definition: Of or relating to theory; abstract; not empirical.

thereafter.definition: After that, from then on; thenceforth.

thereby.definition: By it; by that; by that means, or as a consequence of that.

thread.definition: A long, thin and flexible form of material, generally with a round cross-section, used in sewing, weaving or in the construction of string.

threshold.definition: The bottom-most part of a doorway that one crosses to enter; a sill.

thrilled.definition: To suddenly excite someone, or to give someone great pleasure; to (figuratively) electrify; to experience such a sensation.

thrive.definition: To grow or increase stature; to grow vigorously or luxuriantly, to flourish.

tide.definition: The periodic change of the sea level, particularly when caused by the gravitational influence of the sun and the moon.
